title: oxalis acetosella subsp. griffithii
intro: Oxalis acetosella subsp. griffithii, also known as Griffith's wood sorrel, is a subspecies of the wood sorrel plant that is native to parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an astringent and antimicrobial agent. The health benefits of Oxalis acetosella subsp. griffithii include its potential to help reduce inflammation, treat skin conditions, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
